title: Practice Problem Generator
text: text about the book goes here maybe?

title: 2  README
text:
3 Sample Problem Generator for an Undergraduate Statistics in Psychology Course
An ongoing project to create a series of juypter notebooks .qmd files that can be used to randomly generate sample problems and their worked solutions using python
This readme file needs significant updates

title: 2  README
text:
3.1 Major update
Starting with hypothesis testing, individual .qmd files call the stats_project.py file. The bulk of the code was written when I had very little experience with classes in python and much of this should be restructured. Currently, the code is being adapted to use quarto to render accessible .html documents to meet tile II accessibility guidelines.

title: 2  README
section: 3.2 Documents to produce accessible .html practice problems
text:
3.2 Documents to produce accessible .html practice problems
Each document will produces 30 practice problems in a .html slideshow rendered with revealjs. Each slide is scrollable and contains a single problem. Problems are generated with a randomly determined sample size between 5 and 15 per group. When more than one group, sample size is equivalent between groups.

08 - Hypothesis_Test_z-Scores.qmd: hypothesis testing with z-scores
09 - Single-Sample_t-Tests.qmd: one-sample t-tests
10 - Independent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: independent-samples t-tests
11 - Dependent-Samples_t-Tests.qmd: dependent-samples t-tests
12 - one-way_ANOVA.qmd: one-way ANOVA
13 - repeated-measures_ANOVA: repeated-measures ANOVA
14 - factorial_ANOVA: two-factor ANOVA

title: 3  Hypothesis Testing with z-Scores
text:
import importlib
import stats_project 
import random

# This is to clear cache and reload after edits to the .py file
importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import RandomData 

Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the population mean: \(\mu = {18}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.05}\)





A




21


19


28


21


27


19


26


19


16


23


26


20


20


28


27



The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {22.67}\] \[{\sigma} = {4}\] \[n = {15}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 18\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 18\] 
The decision criteria:  \(z_{crit} = \pm{1.96}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.05}\) Calculate the standard error  \[\sigma_M = \frac{\sigma}{\sqrt{N}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{\sqrt{15}}\]  \[\sigma_M = \frac{4}{3.87}\]  \[\sigma_M = {1.03}\]  Calculate \(z_{obt}\)  \[z_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{\sigma_M}}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{22.67 - 18}{1.03}\]  \[z_{obt} = \frac{4.67}{1.03}\]  \[z_{obt} = {4.53}\]  Calculate Cohen’s d for effect size  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{\sigma}\]  \[d = \frac{22.67 - 18}{4}\]  \[d = \frac{4.67}{4}\]  \[d = {1.17}\]  The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  z = 4.53, p < 0.05, d = 1.17

title: 4  Hypothesis Testing with One-Sample t-tests

import importlib
import stats_project 
import random

# This is to clear cache and reload after edits to the .py file
importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import RandomData 

Given the following data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from \({22}\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\) 





A




32


35


27


28


31


29


39


29


35


30


32


30



 The necessary summary statistics for these data \[M_A = {31.42}\] \[s^2 = {11.9}\] \[n = {12}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu = 22\] \[H_1: \mu \ne 22\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{3.11}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {11}\) Calculate the standard error  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{\frac{11.9}{12}}\]  \[s_M = \sqrt{0.99}\]  \[s_M = {0.99}\]  calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M - \mu}{s_M}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{31.42 - 22}{0.99}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{9.42}{0.99}\]  \[t_{obt} = {9.52}\]  calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M - \mu}{s}\]  \[d = \frac{31.42 - 22}{3.45}\]  \[d = \frac{9.42}{3.45}\]  \[d = {2.73}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(11) = 9.52, p < 0.01, d = 2.73

title: 5  Hypothesis Testing with Independent-Samples t-tests

importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import RandomData 

Given the following between-subjects data, is the mean of \(Group_A\) significantly different from the mean of \(Group_B\)? Use a \({2}\) tailed-test with \(\alpha = {0.01}\)





A
B

 The necessary summary Statistis for these data: \[M_A = {58.27}, M_B = {46.09}\] \[SS_A = {98.18}, SS_B = {136.91}\] \[n_A = {11}, n_B = {11}\]
State the Hypotheses  \[H_0: \mu_A - \mu_B = 0\] \[H_1: \mu_A - \mu_B \ne 0\] 
The decision criteria:  \(t_{crit} = \pm{2.85}, \alpha_{two-tailed} = {0.01}, df = {20}\) Calculate the pooled variance:  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{SS_A + SS_B}{df_A + df_B}}\]  \[s_p^2 = {\frac{98.18 + 136.91}{10 + 10}}\]  \[s_p^2 = \frac{235.09}{20}\]  \[s_p^2 = {11.75}\] 
Calculate the estimated error of the difference between means: \(s_{(M_A - M_B)}\)  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{s_p^2}{n_1} + \frac{s_p^2}{n_1}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{\frac{11.75}{11} + \frac{11.75}{11}}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{1.07 + 1.07}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = \sqrt{2.14}\]  \[s_{(M_A - M_B)} = {1.46}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{(M_A - M_B) - (\mu_A - \mu_B)}{s_{(M_A - M_B)}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{(58.27 - 46.09) - {0}}{1.46}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{12.18}{1.46}\]  \[t_{obt} = {8.34}\] 
Calculate Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_A - M_B}{\sqrt{s_p^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{(58.27 - 46.09)}{{{\sqrt{11.75}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{(12.18)}{3.43}\]  \[d = {3.55}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(20) = 8.34, p < 0.01, d = 3.55

New Summary statistics for the difference scores: \[M_D = {6.12}, \quad \Sigma{D} = {49}, \quad \Sigma{D^2} = {585}\] 
Calculate SS of the difference scores  \[SS_D = \Sigma D^2 - \frac{(\Sigma D)^2}{n}\]  \[SS_D = {585} - \frac{2401}{8}\]  \[SS_D = {585} - {300.12}\]  \[SS_D = {284.88}\] 
Calculate the variance of the difference scores  \[s_D^2 = \frac{SS_D}{df}\]  \[s_D^2 = \frac{284.88}{7}\]  \[s_D^2 = {40.7}\] 
Calculate the estimated standard error of the difference scores  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{s^2}{n}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{\frac{40.7}{8}}\]  \[s_{M_D} = \sqrt{5.09}\]  \[s_{M_D} = {2.26}\] 
Calculate \(t_{obt}\)  \[t_{obt} = {\frac{M_D - \mu_D}{s_{M_D}}}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{6.12 - 0}{2.26}\]  \[t_{obt} = \frac{6.12}{2.26}\]  \[t_{obt} = {2.71}\] 
Calculating Cohen’s d  \[d = \frac{M_D}{\sqrt{s^2}}\]  \[d = \frac{6.12}{{{\sqrt{40.7}}}}\]  \[d = \frac{6.12}{6.38}\]  \[d = {0.96}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  t(7) = 2.71, p < 0.05, d = 0.96

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {714} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {12884} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {40}\) 
\(T_{A} = {135} \quad SS_{A} = {2.88}\)  \(T_{B} = {155} \quad SS_{B} = {7.88}\)  \(T_{C} = {161} \quad SS_{C} = {0.88}\)  \(T_{D} = {125} \quad SS_{D} = {7.88}\)  \(T_{E} = {138} \quad SS_{E} = {9.5}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.91}, \alpha = {0.01}\) 
Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {40} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {39}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {5} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {40} - {5}\]  \[df_{within} = {35}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {12884} - \frac{714^2}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {12884} - \frac{509796}{40}\]  \[SS_{total} = {12884} - {12744.9}\]  \[SS_{total} = {139.1}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {2.88 + 7.88 + 0.88 + 7.88 + 9.5}\]  \[SS_{within} = {29.02}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {139.1} - {29.02}\]  \[SS_{between} = {110.08}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{110.08}{4}\]  \[MS_{between} = {27.52}\] 
\[MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}\]  \[MS_{within} = \frac{29.02}{35}\]  \[MS_{within} = {0.83}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{27.52}{0.83}\]  \[F_{obt} = {33.16}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}\]  \[\eta^2 = \frac{110.08}{139.1}\]  \[\eta^2 = {0.79}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({4}, {35}) = {33.16}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.79}\)

Summary statistics for these data: \(G = {3059} \quad \Sigma X^2 = {176897} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {56}\) 
\(T_{A} = {703} \quad SS_{A} = {1796.36}\)  \(T_{B} = {964} \quad SS_{B} = {1453.71}\)  \(T_{C} = {673} \quad SS_{C} = {956.93}\)  \(T_{D} = {719} \quad SS_{D} = {1733.21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {2.85}, \alpha = {0.05}\) 
Stage 1 Calculations:  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{total} = N - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {56} - 1\]  \[df_{total} = {55}\]  \[df_{between} = k - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {4} - 1\]  \[df_{between} = {3}\]  \[df_{within} = N - K\]  \[df_{within} = {56} - {4}\]  \[df_{within} = {52}\] 
Calculate the Sum of Squares  \[SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{total} = {176897} - \frac{3059^2}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {176897} - \frac{9357481}{56}\]  \[SS_{total} = {176897} - {167097.88}\]  \[SS_{total} = {9799.12}\]  \[SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\]  \[SS_{within} = {1796.36 + 1453.71 + 956.93 + 1733.21}\]  \[SS_{within} = {5940.21}\]  \[SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\]  \[SS_{between} = {9799.12} - {5940.21}\]  \[SS_{between} = {3858.91}\]  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \[SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\] 
Stage 2 Calculations:  Partition the Degrees of Freedom  \[df_{subjects} = n - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {14} - 1\]  \[df_{subjects} = {13}\]  \[df_{error} = df_{within} - df_{subjects}\]  \[df_{error} = {52} - {13}\]  \[df_{error} = {39}\]

\[SS_{subjects} = \Sigma{\frac{P^2}{k}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{232^2}{4} + \frac{217^2}{4} + \frac{206^2}{4} + \frac{200^2}{4} + \frac{212^2}{4} + \frac{197^2}{4} + \frac{210^2}{4} + \frac{219^2}{4} + \frac{253^2}{4} + \frac{218^2}{4} + \frac{210^2}{4} + \frac{238^2}{4} + \frac{220^2}{4} + \frac{227^2}{4}} - \frac{3059^2}{56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {\frac{53824}{4} + \frac{47089}{4} + \frac{42436}{4} + \frac{40000}{4} + \frac{44944}{4} + \frac{38809}{4} + \frac{44100}{4} + \frac{47961}{4} + \frac{64009}{4} + \frac{47524}{4} + \frac{44100}{4} + \frac{56644}{4} + \frac{48400}{4} + \frac{51529}{4}} - \frac{9357481}{56}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {{13456.0} + {11772.25} + {10609.0} + {10000.0} + {11236.0} + {9702.25} + {11025.0} + {11990.25} + {16002.25} + {11881.0} + {11025.0} + {14161.0} + {12100.0} + {12882.25}} - {167097.88}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {167842.25} - {167097.88}\]  \[SS_{subjects} = {744.37}\]  \[SS_{error} = SS_{within} - SS_{subjects}\]  \[SS_{error} = {5940.21} - {744.37}\]  \[SS_{error} = {5195.84}\] 
Calculate the Mean Squares  \[MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}\]  \[MS_{between} = \frac{3858.91}{3}\]  \[MS_{between} = {1286.3}\] 
\[MS_{error} = \frac{SS_{error}}{df_{error}}\]  \[MS_{error} = \frac{5195.84}{39}\]  \[MS_{error} = {133.23}\] 
Calculate the F-Ratio  \[F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{error}}\]  \[F_{obt} = \frac{1286.3}{133.23}\]  \[F_{obt} = {9.65}\] 
Calculate \(\eta^2\)  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total} - SS_{subjects}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{3858.91}{{9799.12} - {744.37}}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = \frac{3858.91}{9054.75}\]  \[\eta_p^2 = {0.43}\] 
The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({3}, {39}) = {9.65}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.43}\)

title: 9  Two-Factor ANOVA
text:
import importlib
import stats_project 
import random

# This is to clear cache and reload after edits to the .py file
importlib.reload(stats_project)
from stats_project import FactorialData

Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with \(\alpha = {0.05}\) to analyze the following data  Full Group Summary Data  \(T_{A1B1} = {124} \quad M_{A1B1} = {17.71}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {49.43} \quad n_{A1B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B2} = {109} \quad M_{A1B2} = {15.57}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {27.71} \quad n_{A1B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A1B3} = {106} \quad M_{A1B3} = {15.14}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {158.86} \quad n_{A1B3} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B1} = {150} \quad M_{A2B1} = {21.43}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {71.71} \quad n_{A2B1} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B2} = {128} \quad M_{A2B2} = {18.29}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {49.43} \quad n_{A2B2} = {7}\)  \(T_{A2B3} = {98} \quad M_{A2B3} = {14.0}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {12.0} \quad n_{A2B3} = {7}\)   Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations  Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: \(df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}\)  \(df_{total} = N - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {42} - 1\)  \(df_{total} = {41}\)  \(df_{between} = k - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {6} - 1\)  \(df_{between} = {5}\)  \(df_{within} = N - K\)  \(df_{within} = {42} - {6}\)  \(df_{within} = {36}\) 
Calculate the Sum of Squares: \(SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}\)  \(SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12795} - \frac{715^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12795} - \frac{511225}{42}\)  \(SS_{total} = {12795} - {12172.02}\)  \(SS_{total} = {622.98}\)  \(SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}\)  \(SS_{within} = {49.43 + 27.71 + 158.86 + 71.71 + 49.43 + 12.0}\)  \(SS_{within} = {369.14}\)  \(SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}\)  \(SS_{between} = {622.98} - {369.14}\)  \(SS_{between} = {253.84}\)  note: the other way to calculate \(SS_{betwen}\) is:  \(SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\) 
Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_A\) Summary Data for Factor_A  \(T_{A_1} = {339}  \quad n_{A_1} = {21}\)  \(T_{A_2} = {376}  \quad n_{A_2} = {21}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2\)  \(H_1: \mu_1 \ne \mu_2\)  partition df for \(Factor_A\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {2} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_A} = {1}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {4.11}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_A\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_A}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{339}^2}{21} + \frac{{376}^2}{21}} - \frac{{715}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{114921}}{21} + \frac{{141376}}{21}} - \frac{{511225}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_A} = {{5472.43} + {6732.19}} - {12172.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {12204.62} - {12172.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_A} = {32.6}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_A}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{32.6}{1}\)  \(MS_{Factor_A} = {32.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_A}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = \frac{32.6}{10.25}\)  \(F_{Factor_A} = {3.18}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{32.6}{{622.98} - {175.19} - {46.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{32.6}{401.74}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({1}, {36}) = {3.18}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.08}\) Stage 2 calculations for \(Factor_B\) Summary Data for Factor_B  \(T_{B_1} = {274}  \quad n_{B_1} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_2} = {237}  \quad n_{B_2} = {14}\)  \(T_{B_3} = {204}  \quad n_{B_3} = {14}\)   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3\)  \(H_1:\) At least one mean is different  partition df for \(Factor_B\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = k_{Factor_B} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1\)  \(df_{Factor_B} = {2}\)   The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(Factor_B\) Calculate \(SS_{Factor_B}\)  \(\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{274}^2}{14} + \frac{{237}^2}{14} + \frac{{204}^2}{14}} - \frac{{715}^2}{42}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{75076}}{14} + \frac{{56169}}{14} + \frac{{41616}}{14}} - \frac{{511225}}{42}\) \(SS_{Factor_B} = {{5362.57} + {4012.07} + {2972.57}} - {12172.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {12347.21} - {12172.02}\)  \(SS_{Factor_B} = {175.19}\)  Mean Squares for \({Factor_B}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{175.19}{2}\)  \(MS_{Factor_B} = {87.6}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({Factor_B}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = \frac{87.6}{10.25}\)  \(F_{Factor_B} = {8.55}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{175.19}{{622.98} - {32.6} - {46.05}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{175.19}{544.33}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.32}\) The results:  reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {8.55}, p < {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.32}\)  Stage 2 calculations for the \(AxB\) interaction   State the Hypotheses  \(H_0:\) There is no AxB interation  \(H_1:\) There is an AxB interaction  Partition df for the interaction  \(df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {5} - {1} - {2}\)  \(df_{AxB} = {2}\) 
 The decision criteria:  \(F_{crit} = {3.26}, \alpha = {0.05}\)  Partition the SS for \(AxB\) Calculate \(SS_{AxB}\)  \(SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}\) \(SS_{AxB} = {253.84} - {32.6} - {175.19}\)  \(SS_{AxB} = {46.05}\)  Mean Squares for \({AxB}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = \frac{46.05}{2}\)  \(MS_{AxB} = {23.02}\)
Calculate the F-Ratio for \({AxB}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}\)  \(F_{AxB} = \frac{23.02}{10.25}\)  \(F_{AxB} = {2.25}\) 
 Calculate \(\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB\)  \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{46.05}{{622.98} - {32.6} - {175.19}}\) \(\eta_p^2 = \frac{46.05}{415.19}\) \(\eta_p^2 = {0.11}\) The results:  fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,  \(F({2}, {36}) = {2.25}, p > {0.05}, \eta_p^2 = {0.11}\)
